patterns.name: Labeled Form Field
patterns.intent: Collect a short value with an accessible label, helper copy, and optional error.
selectionRules: Use Input for one-line values.
Use Textarea when the expected answer is longer than one sentence.
Never rely on placeholder as the only label.
patterns.jsx: We only use this for account updates.

patterns.name: Dialog Confirmation
patterns.intent: Ask the user to confirm a consequential action without losing page context.
patterns.composition: Dialog with DialogContent: title, concise description, footer with a secondary outline Button and a destructive Button.
selectionRules: Use Dialog for blocking decisions.
Use the destructive variant for destructive confirmation.
Avoid long multi-step flows inside Dialog.
patterns.jsx: <Dialog defaultOpen><DialogContent><DialogHeader><DialogTitle>Delete project?</DialogTitle><DialogDescription>This permanently removes the project and its data.</DialogDescription></DialogHeader><DialogFooter><DialogClose asChild><Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button></DialogClose><Button variant="destructive">Delete</Button></DialogFooter></DialogContent></Dialog>

patterns.name: Object Summary Card
patterns.intent: Represent one repeated entity in a collection.
patterns.composition: Card with header title/status, content metadata (DataList), footer actions.
selectionRules: Use one card per object.
Avoid nested cards.
Use Table when row/column comparison matters more than object browsing.
patterns.jsx: Acme Inc.Active

patterns.name: Persistent Feedback
patterns.intent: Keep status visible until the underlying condition changes.
patterns.composition: Alert with an icon, AlertTitle, AlertDescription, and an optional action nearby.
selectionRules: Use Alert for persistent feedback.
Use Toast/Toaster (sonner) only when the message can disappear.
Match the variant to severity (destructive for errors).
patterns.jsx: <Alert className="w-96"><TriangleAlert /><AlertTitle>Payment method expiring</AlertTitle><AlertDescription>Update your card before the next billing cycle.</AlertDescription></Alert>

patterns.name: Page Header with Actions
patterns.intent: Title a view and expose its primary and secondary actions.
patterns.composition: Flex space-between: left stack with Heading + supporting Text, right row of actions with one default (primary) Button.
selectionRules: Expose at most one default (primary) action per header.
Use Button for actions and Link/Breadcrumb for navigation.
Keep the supporting line to one sentence.
patterns.jsx: Team membersManage who can access this workspace.

patterns.name: Empty State
patterns.intent: Explain why a region is empty and offer the next action.
patterns.composition: EmptyState with an icon, title, description, and a single recovery action.
selectionRules: Give one clear next action, not several.
Use neutral, non-alarming copy for normal-empty (not error) states.
Reserve for genuinely empty regions, not loading (use Skeleton/Spinner).
patterns.jsx: <EmptyState icon={<Search size={28} />} title="No results found" description="Try adjusting your filters or search terms." action={<Button>Clear filters</Button>} />

patterns.name: Search and Filter Bar
patterns.intent: Let the user narrow a collection by free text and a category.
patterns.composition: Flex row: InputGroup with a leading search icon (flex-1) + a NativeSelect for the category facet.
selectionRules: Always give the search Input an accessible label.
Use Select/NativeSelect for finite facets, not free text.
Keep the search field the widest element in the row.
patterns.jsx: }>

patterns.name: Metric Summary Row
patterns.intent: Show a few headline numbers side by side at the top of a dashboard.
patterns.composition: Flex row of equal-width Cards, each wrapping a Stat with label + value.
selectionRules: Keep to 2-4 metrics; more belongs in a Table.
Use the Stat component for the label/value pair (no bare spans).
Format numbers consistently.
patterns.jsx: {[["Revenue","$48.2k"],["Active users","1,284"],["Churn","2.1%"]].map(([label,value]) => ())}

patterns.name: Form Layout
patterns.intent: Collect several related fields with labels, then submit.
patterns.composition: Card with header, a grid of labeled fields in the content, and footer actions (secondary + primary).
selectionRules: One primary submit Button; secondary actions outline.
Every field has a Label tied by htmlFor/id.
Use Textarea for multi-line answers.
patterns.jsx: Create accountFill in your details to get started.

patterns.name: Table with Toolbar
patterns.intent: Browse a collection with search, a facet filter, and an action.
patterns.composition: Toolbar row (search InputGroup flex-1 + NativeSelect facet + primary Button) above a Table; status cells use Badge variants.
selectionRules: Use Table when row/column comparison matters more than browsing objects.
Give the search Input an accessible label.
Use status Badge variants (success/warning) for state cells.
patterns.jsx:
}>
InvoiceStatusAmountINV-001Paid$250.00INV-002Pending$150.00

patterns.name: Settings Section
patterns.intent: Group related preference toggles with labels and descriptions.
patterns.composition: Card with a titled header and rows of (label + description, Switch) separated by Separator.
selectionRules: Use Switch for instant on/off settings (no save needed).
Separate rows with Separator, not nested cards.
Pair each control with a short description.
patterns.jsx:
NotificationsChoose what you want to hear about.

Email

Account activity and security

Push

Reminders and product news

patterns.name: Auth Card
patterns.intent: Sign in or sign up in a focused, centered card.
patterns.composition: Narrow Card with title/description, stacked labeled fields, a full-width primary Button, and a link action.
selectionRules: One full-width primary action.
Use type=password / type=email for the right keyboards.
Secondary actions as variant=link.
patterns.jsx: Sign inWelcome back — enter your details.

patterns.name: Loading State
patterns.intent: Hold layout with skeletons while content loads.
patterns.composition: The destination layout rebuilt from Skeleton blocks matching the real content's shape.
selectionRules: Use Skeleton for known-shape content; Spinner for unknown-duration waits.
Match skeleton sizes to the real content to avoid layout shift.
Not for empty results (use the Empty State pattern).

patterns.name: Error State
patterns.intent: Explain a failure and offer a recovery action.
patterns.composition: A destructive Alert (icon + title + cause) with a retry Button nearby.
selectionRules: Use the destructive Alert variant for failures.
Always offer a next action (retry / contact).
Keep the cause specific and non-blaming.
patterns.jsx: Couldn't load dataSomething went wrong while fetching. Check your connection and try again.
